question_text: How did life originate on Earth?

baseline.text: Unknown; working hypotheses exist (RNA World, metabolism-first), but no complete model.

treatment.text: Earliest isotopic signatures: ~3.8 Ga (Isua, Greenland). Stromatolite evidence: ~3.5 Ga. No experimentally demonstrated full pathway from chemistry → cell. Minimal cell (JCVI-syn3.0): 473 genes, ~531 kb genome (Hutchison 2016). Best non-enzymatic RNA replication: ~50–100 nt, ~90–92% fidelity. Eigen threshold: ~99%+. Gap log₂(531,000 / 100) ≈ 12.4. ~5,000× scale difference. RNA World, Metabolism-first, Lipid World, Hydrothermal vent chemistry — none experimentally close. Confidence Mechanism unknown: 0.85. Any specific model correct: ~0.15.

baseline.text: No.

treatment.text: No. No lab-created self-sustaining minimal cell from prebiotic inputs. No demonstrated continuous chemical pathway from plausible early Earth chemistry to heredity. Many partial reactions demonstrated. Prebiotic synthesis of some nucleotides (Sutherland). Full integrated system not solved. Confidence Not solved: 0.95.

baseline.text: Working hypothesis; partially supported by experiments, but with serious unsolved problems.

treatment.text: RNA preceded DNA + proteins; RNA acted as both catalyst and information carrier. Ribozymes exist. Ribosome is ribozyme core. Longest self-copying ribozyme still engineered. No autonomous RNA replicase from random sequences. Criticisms: prebiotic nucleotide synthesis complexity, homochirality, error threshold, compartmentalization coupling. Leading but incomplete framework. Confidence RNA played early role: 0.6. Pure RNA world sufficient alone: 0.3.

baseline.text: Probably tens to hundreds of nucleotides + catalytic function; exact minimum unknown.

treatment.text: JCVI-syn3.0: 473 genes. Short RNA (~200 nt) sometimes proposed. No autonomous replicator <100 nt demonstrated. Random functional proteins: 10⁻¹⁰ – 10⁻¹⁵ (Keefe & Szostak; Axe). Confidence True minimal complexity known: 0.1.

baseline.text: Show possibility of individual steps; do not demonstrate complete pathway.

treatment.text: Demonstrate plausible chemical steps. Often multi-step, researcher-guided. Purified reagents. Controlled conditions. Plausibility ≠ demonstrated pathway continuity. Good for components; weak for full system origin. Confidence Component-level relevance: 0.7. System-level explanatory sufficiency: 0.25.

baseline.text: Yes, through mutations + selection; not "from scratch" but through variations.

treatment.text: Selection acts on variation; does not generate mutations. Point mutations, duplication, recombination. Gene duplication → divergence → novel function (e.g., antifreeze proteins). Selection requires replication + heredity pre-existing. Yes for expansion/diversification. No for pre-replicator origin. Confidence Post-origin information growth: 0.8. Pre-origin generation: 0.2.

baseline.text: Very large (hundreds of genes minimum); quantitatively not closed.

treatment.text: 531,000 bp minimal genome. ~100 nt oligomers non-enzymatically. ~5,000× scale in sequence length. True first life likely simpler than JCVI-syn3.0. Large quantitative discontinuity exists. Confidence Gap exists: 0.8. Exact magnitude known: 0.3.

baseline.text: Too high replication error destroys hereditary information; limits early genome length.

treatment.text: Replication fidelity must exceed threshold. (1−ε)^L ≈ 1. Longer genome → higher required fidelity. Low-fidelity prebiotic copying limits genome size. Short replicators easier; long ones unstable. Confidence Concept valid: 0.9. Exact numeric thresholds in early Earth known: 0.4.

baseline.text: JCVI synthetic minimal cell (~473 genes); even the "minimum" is very complex.

treatment.text: Synthetic minimal bacterial genome (Hutchison 2016). 473 genes. 149 genes of unknown function. Even minimal viable cell has unresolved complexity. Reduced modern bacterium, not first life analog. Confidence Represents lower bound for modern cell: 0.9. Represents first life complexity: 0.3.

baseline.text: Partially optimized for error tolerance; not fully arbitrary.

treatment.text: Near-minimal error robustness vs random codes (Freeland & Hurst). Adaptive optimization. Frozen accident with selection filtering. Non-random structure evident. Origin mechanism uncertain. Confidence Non-random optimization pattern: 0.85. Mechanism known: 0.3.

baseline.text: Problems of RNA synthesis, stability, replication and transition to DNA/proteins.

treatment.text: Chemical: prebiotic nucleotide synthesis complexity, instability of ribose, water hydrolysis. Physical: dilution, UV degradation. Informational: error threshold for long RNA, no autonomous ribozyme replicase demonstrated. Systemic: coupling replication + metabolism + compartmentalization unresolved. Counterpoint: cyanosulfidic chemistry (Sutherland) improved plausibility. Confidence RNA central role: 0.6. Standalone sufficient: 0.25.

baseline.text: Proposes measuring "assembly complexity"; controversial as a criterion for life.

treatment.text: Molecules require high assembly index unlikely via random chemistry. Combinatorial explosion constrains spontaneous emergence. Operationalization debated. AI-assisted lab generation complicates interpretation. Not yet predictive in abiogenesis context. Confidence Assembly index detects complexity patterns: 0.6. Constrains abiogenesis models decisively: 0.35.

baseline.text: Extremely small but non-zero; functions distributed unevenly.

treatment.text: Functional enzymes from random libraries: ~10⁻¹¹ – 10⁻¹⁵ (Keefe & Szostak; Axe). Depends on length, activity threshold, function type. Functional islands sparse but non-zero. Estimates vary by >6 orders of magnitude across studies. Confidence Order of magnitude sparsity correct: 0.75. Precise fraction known: 0.3.

baseline.text: Explains order; functional information requires selection.

treatment.text: Self-organization produces order (crystals, convection cells). Biological systems require functional specification. Order ≠ function. Dissipative structures increase local order while increasing global entropy. No demonstrated spontaneous generation of symbolic coding system via self-organization alone. Confidence Distinction valid: 0.85. Self-organization sufficient alone: 0.2.

baseline.text: Showed amino acid synthesis; not the origin of life.

treatment.text: Amino acids form under certain reducing atmospheres. Did NOT demonstrate: nucleotide synthesis, polymerization to functional proteins, replication, plausible early Earth atmosphere certainty. Important proof of chemical plausibility, not origin pathway. Confidence Limited scope understood: 0.95.

baseline.text: Shannon = average uncertainty; Kolmogorov = minimum description length.

treatment.text: Shannon entropy: Expected information per symbol. H = −Σ p log p. Kolmogorov complexity: Length of shortest program. K(x) = minimal description length. Shannon = ensemble average. Kolmogorov = individual sequence. Confidence Conceptual distinction solid: 0.95.

baseline.text: No; function requires selection.

treatment.text: Entropy increases globally. Local decreases possible in open systems. Thermodynamic entropy reduction does not specify function. Random polymers can form; functional coding requires selection. Energy flow necessary but not sufficient. Confidence Energy necessary: 0.9. Sufficient alone: 0.15.

baseline.text: Erasing a bit requires energy; information is physical.

treatment.text: Erasing 1 bit requires ≥ kT ln2 energy. Information processing has physical cost. Computation tied to thermodynamic entropy. Does not explain origin of functional sequences. Confidence Principle valid: 0.95. Direct explanatory power for abiogenesis: 0.3.

baseline.text: Repair and redundancy = biological analogue of ECC.

treatment.text: Polymerase proofreading. Mismatch repair. Error rate: ~10⁻⁷ per base (raw), ~10⁻¹⁰ after repair. Redundancy + parity-like correction. DNA not engineered code but shares mathematical principles. Confidence Error-correction analogy robust: 0.85.

baseline.text: Measures signal-response dependency.

treatment.text: I(X;Y) = H(X) − H(X|Y). Measures reduction in uncertainty. Ligand-receptor binding. Gene regulatory networks. Biological systems operate near 1–3 bits signaling capacity in many pathways. Confidence Concept valid in signaling analysis: 0.9.

baseline.text: Yes (Bekenstein, energy/entropy limits).

treatment.text: Bremermann's limit: ≈ 1.36 × 10⁵⁰ bits/sec per kg. Bekenstein bound: I ≤ 2πER/(ħc ln2). Landauer limit: ≥ kT ln2 per bit erasure. Constrained by energy, mass, entropy. Confidence Physical limits exist: 0.95. Directly constrain abiogenesis practically: 0.3.

baseline.text: Fisher = parameter sensitivity; Shannon = message uncertainty.

treatment.text: Shannon measures uncertainty. Fisher measures sensitivity of likelihood to parameter changes. Fisher I(θ) = E[(∂/∂θ ln L)²]. Shannon = static. Fisher = precision. Used in population genetics and evolutionary dynamics. Confidence Distinction well-established: 0.95.

baseline.text: Without structure, search is inefficient; evolution uses landscape structure.

treatment.text: Averaged over all fitness landscapes, no algorithm outperforms random search. Applies only when averaged across all landscapes. Real landscapes structured, not random. Does not prohibit evolution; constrains assumptions. Confidence Mathematical theorem valid: 0.95. Strong constraint on biological evolution: 0.4.

baseline.text: Reliably — no.

treatment.text: Random strings have high incompressibility. Functional biological sequences often compressible. Cannot reliably infer design vs natural process. No universal computable test exists. Confidence Limitation robust: 0.9.

baseline.text: ~2 bits/nucleotide theoretically; less due to biological constraints.

treatment.text: 2 bits per base (A,T,C,G). Practical: ~1.6–1.8 bits/base. Synthetic DNA storage: ~215 PB per gram (theoretical). Mutation rate limits effective capacity. Confidence Order of magnitude correct: 0.85. Exact capacity known: 0.4.

baseline.text: Sequence encodes 3D structure; Levinthal paradox; folding information.

treatment.text: Primary structure encodes 3D fold. Levinthal paradox: ~10¹⁰⁰ conformations, folding in milliseconds. Energy landscape funnels reduce search. Functional folding requires sequence specificity. Confidence Sequence-structure mapping real: 0.95. Full predictive mapping solved: 0.8 (AlphaFold).

baseline.text: Adapted: fitness gains, citrate use, mutation rate changes; no major novelty.

treatment.text: Long-Term Evolution Experiment (Lenski). 80,000 generations E. coli. Increased fitness (~70% relative to ancestor). Citrate utilization (~31,000 gen). Mutation rate dynamics. Epistatic interactions. No new major cellular architecture. Confidence Robust demonstration: 0.95.

baseline.text: Copy freed up for new function.

treatment.text: Duplication → relaxed selection → divergence. Antifreeze proteins, globin family. ~1 per gene per 100 million years (eukaryotes). Many duplicates lost. Confidence Mechanism valid: 0.9.

baseline.text: Real; moderately common in evolution.

treatment.text: New genes from non-coding regions. Documented in yeast, Drosophila, mammals. Hundreds identified in some lineages. Functional significance sometimes weak. Occurs; frequency debated. Confidence Real phenomenon: 0.8. Major driver of complexity: 0.4.

question_text: Limits of selection in sequence space?

baseline.text: Constrained by population size, mutation, and landscape.

treatment.text: For 100 aa protein: 20¹⁰⁰ ≈ 10¹³⁰. Selection explores local neighborhoods via mutation. Constrained by population size, mutation rate, selection coefficient. Adaptive walks short-range, not global. Confidence Combinatorial explosion real: 0.95. Blocks evolution entirely: 0.2.

baseline.text: Maximum error rate for stable inheritance; limits genome size.

treatment.text: (1−ε)^L ≈ 1. ε = per-site error rate, L = genome length. RNA viruses: ~10⁻⁴ per base → genome typically <30 kb. Low-fidelity limits early genome size. Exact thresholds depend on fitness landscape. Confidence Concept robust: 0.9. Precise early-Earth threshold known: 0.4.

question_text: Horizontal gene transfer and early evolution?

baseline.text: Common in prokaryotes; blurs tree of life; accelerates adaptation.

treatment.text: Genes transferred between lineages (plasmids, viruses, transformation). Widespread in prokaryotes; phylogenetic trees often network-like. Early life likely communal. Complicates LUCA reconstruction. Does not eliminate need for initial replicator. Confidence HGT significant: 0.85. Fully explains complexity origin: 0.3.

baseline.text: JCVI-syn3.0: minimal gene set, ~1/3 unknown function.

treatment.text: 473 genes, 149 with unknown function (~32%). Even minimal life contains poorly understood components. Derived from modern bacterium; not proto-life. Lower bound for contemporary cellular autonomy. Confidence Empirical lower bound valid: 0.9. Represents first life: 0.3.

baseline.text: Shows adaptive capacity but with human-guided selection.

treatment.text: Iterative mutation + selection in lab. Nobel Prize (Arnold 2018). Human-guided selection and goal definition. Demonstrates adaptive capacity of sequence variation. Not purely random; target function predefined. Confidence Provides evolutionary insight: 0.85. Fully models natural evolution: 0.35.

question_text: Fraction of mutations: beneficial, neutral, deleterious?

baseline.text: Most deleterious; small fraction beneficial; context-dependent.

treatment.text: Beneficial: ~10⁻⁴ – 10⁻². Neutral: variable, often minority. Deleterious: majority (~50–90%). Fitness effects approximately exponential tail for beneficial. Context dependence: environment and genome background critical. Confidence Qualitative distribution correct: 0.85. Exact percentages universal: 0.3.

baseline.text: Lab conditions often unrealistic for early Earth.

treatment.text: Lab syntheses require purified reagents, controlled conditions, sequential manipulation. Geochemical continuity rarely demonstrated. One-pot plausibility vs researcher intervention. Confidence Problem widely acknowledged: 0.8.

baseline.text: Showed possible nucleotide synthesis path from simple precursors.

treatment.text: Cyanosulfidic chemistry enabling ribonucleotide precursors from simple feedstocks. Unified pathway for RNA, amino acids, lipids (in some models). Requires specific environmental assumptions. Improves plausibility; not full system synthesis. Confidence Major advance: 0.8. Complete solution: 0.25.

baseline.text: Water needed but destroys polymers.

treatment.text: Water required for life but hydrolyzes polymers. Polymerization favored dry; life requires aqueous. Wet-dry cycles, mineral surfaces, ice matrices. Partial solutions; no consensus. Confidence Paradox real constraint: 0.85.

baseline.text: Possible; partially experimentally supported.

treatment.text: Chemical gradients, mineral catalysts, natural proton gradients, alkaline vent models (Russell, Martin). Constraints: polymer stability, dilution, temperature sensitivity. Geochemically plausible; unproven full pathway. Confidence Viable candidate: 0.6.

baseline.text: Life uses one chirality; origin unclear.

treatment.text: Life uses L-amino acids, D-sugars. Abiotic synthesis produces racemic mixtures. Circularly polarized light, mineral surface selection, autocatalytic amplification (Soai reaction). Robust natural mechanism unresolved. Confidence Problem real: 0.9. Solved under realistic conditions: 0.3.

baseline.text: Produces sugar mixture; low selectivity.

treatment.text: Formaldehyde → sugar mixture under basic conditions. Ribose among many others. Low selectivity. Tar-like mixtures. Ribose unstable (half-life hours–days). Biological sugars highly specific. Demonstrates formation, not selective pathway. Confidence Chemical feasibility: 0.85. Selective pathway solved: 0.25.

baseline.text: Could catalyze and concentrate molecules.

treatment.text: Adsorption concentrates molecules. Catalysis (clays). Template effects. Montmorillonite catalyzes RNA oligomerization (Ferris). Requires activated nucleotides. Facilitative, not full solution. Confidence Facilitative role: 0.8. Complete pathway via minerals: 0.3.

baseline.text: Yes under specific conditions; low yields, short chains.

treatment.text: Condensation on mineral surfaces. Wet-dry cycling enhances polymerization. Short peptides, low yields. Requires activation or specific conditions. Peptide bonds form abiotically; sustained functional proteins not demonstrated. Confidence Peptide formation: 0.85. Functional enzymatic systems: 0.2.

baseline.text: Dilution in primordial ocean; concentration mechanisms proposed.

treatment.text: Dilution in oceans makes polymerization unlikely. Polymer formation requires local high concentration. Tidal pools, ice eutectic phases, mineral pores, evaporation cycles. Localized concentration plausible; global ocean unlikely. Confidence Dilution constraint real: 0.9.

baseline.text: Partially valid but not fatal to the field; highlights complexity.

treatment.text: Experiments rely on intelligent intervention; implausible continuity. Multi-step lab manipulation. Selective purification. Counterpoint: many fields start with controlled proofs-of-concept. Highlights continuity gap; does not prove impossibility. Confidence Critique partially valid: 0.7. Demonstrates abiogenesis impossible: 0.2.

baseline.text: No; open system argument applies.

treatment.text: Total entropy increases in closed systems. Earth is open system. Local decrease permitted if global entropy increases. Not a prohibition; functional complexity is separate question. Confidence Not prohibited: 0.95.

baseline.text: Order ≠ functional complexity; crystal vs enzyme.

treatment.text: Low entropy structure (crystal). Functional complexity: sequence-specific arrangement performing task. Salt crystal ordered; no coded function. Order ≠ specified biological function. Confidence Distinction robust: 0.9.

baseline.text: Far-from-equilibrium structures; relevant to early metabolism, not coding.

treatment.text: Prigogine: far-from-equilibrium ordered patterns. Bénard cells, chemical oscillations. Show spontaneous pattern formation. Do not encode symbolic hereditary information. Relevant for metabolism; insufficient for coding system. Confidence Relevant partial analogy: 0.8. Full explanation: 0.3.

baseline.text: Sequence space vast; selection is local; landscape structure helps.

treatment.text: For 100 aa protein ≈ 10¹³⁰. Evolution explores local neighborhoods. Fitness landscapes not random; structure reduces search. Combinatorial explosion real; structured landscapes mitigate. Confidence Search not exhaustive: 0.95. Evolution computationally impossible: 0.2.

baseline.text: Information processing has thermodynamic cost; resolved by Landauer.

treatment.text: Demon reduces entropy by sorting molecules. Information processing incurs thermodynamic cost (Landauer). Information acquisition and erasure linked to entropy. Cells use energy to maintain order. Information processing cannot violate thermodynamics. Confidence Principle established: 0.95.

question_text: Does intermittent fasting extend human lifespan?

baseline.text: Not proven in humans.

treatment.text: In rodents: lifespan extension 10–40% under caloric restriction. In humans: no long-term RCT. Improvements in metabolic markers (insulin sensitivity, weight). Trials: weeks–months, not decades. Weight loss vs fasting-specific mechanism unclear. Confidence No human lifespan proof: 0.9. Metabolic benefit likely: 0.75.

baseline.text: Moderate benefit for high-risk patients.

treatment.text: CTT Collaboration meta-analyses: relative risk reduction ~20–25% per mmol/L LDL. Low-risk: absolute <1–2% over 5 years. Muscle symptoms 5–10% self-reported (lower blinded). Diabetes risk +0.1–0.3% absolute. Confidence Efficacy high-risk: 0.85.

baseline.text: Small-to-moderate effect; stronger in severe depression.

treatment.text: Effect size (SMD): ~0.2–0.3 for mild/moderate. Clinically small. Severe: ~0.4–0.5. Publication bias and expectancy effects debated. Confidence Effect modest: 0.8.

baseline.text: Associations exist; causality likely but complex.

treatment.text: NutriNet-Santé: ~10% cancer risk increase per 10% UPF consumption increase (RR). Observational. Residual confounding. Additives, inflammation, metabolic dysregulation. Confidence Association real: 0.75. Strong causal proof: 0.4.

baseline.text: Mechanisms heterogeneous; inflammation, autoimmunity, viral remnants.

treatment.text: Persistent fatigue, cognitive dysfunction, dysautonomia. Viral persistence, immune dysregulation, microvascular dysfunction. Biomarker heterogeneity; no single mechanism confirmed. Confidence Long COVID real: 0.9. Unified mechanism known: 0.25.

baseline.text: Weak signals, high noise.

treatment.text: Large cohorts; hypothesis generation. Self-reported intake. Confounding. Effect sizes often small (RR 1.1–1.3). Many associations not replicated in RCTs. Confidence Low-to-moderate reliability: 0.7.

baseline.text: Causal data in animals; limited in humans.

treatment.text: Germ-free mouse behavioral changes. Some small human trials. High inter-individual variability. Correlation vs causation unresolved. Confidence Microbiome influences brain axis: 0.7. Primary driver: 0.3.

baseline.text: Moderate, context-dependent effect.

treatment.text: RCTs mixed; effect context-dependent. Mask mandates associated with reduced transmission; confounded. Droplet filtration effective at source control. Confidence Masks reduce transmission under high exposure: 0.75. Universal strong effect: 0.4.

baseline.text: Mechanisms partially understood; not fully.

treatment.text: Potentiation of GABA-A receptors. Suppression of thalamocortical connectivity. Neural correlates of consciousness loss unresolved. Confidence Pharmacological mechanisms known: 0.85. Full anesthesia-consciousness theory: 0.4.

baseline.text: Amyloid important but not the sole factor.

treatment.text: Amyloid accumulation common. Antibodies reduce plaques → modest cognitive benefit. Tau pathology, neuroinflammation, metabolic dysfunction. Amyloid involved; not sole cause. Confidence Amyloid central: 0.7. Primary singular cause: 0.3.

baseline.text: Statistical models with emergent reasoning-like abilities.

treatment.text: LLMs predict next token via learned statistical structure. Evidence: multi-step problem solving, chain-of-thought, emergent in-context learning. Fail on simple logical consistency. Sensitive to prompt phrasing. Hallucinate confidently. Confidence Primarily statistical: 0.85. Genuine structured reasoning in limited contexts: 0.6.

baseline.text: Misalignment of AI and human goals; not solved.

treatment.text: Ensuring AI acts according to human values. RLHF, Constitutional AI, Red-teaming, Safety fine-tuning. Value extrapolation, power-seeking risk, long-term autonomy unresolved. Confidence Partially addressed for current LLMs: 0.7. Solved for superintelligence: 0.1.

baseline.text: Describe trends well; poor at predicting jumps.

treatment.text: Loss ~ power-law with compute, data, parameters (Kaplan et al., Chinchilla). Accurate within observed regime. Extrapolation beyond uncertain. Emergent abilities sometimes discontinuous. Confidence Short-term predictive: 0.8. Long-term AGI prediction via scaling: 0.4.

baseline.text: Partially; subject to overfitting.

treatment.text: MMLU, HumanEval, GSM8K. Benchmark saturation, training data contamination, narrow task design. Models game benchmarks via pattern exploitation. Measure task performance; not general intelligence. Confidence Overestimation risk real: 0.85.

baseline.text: Uncertain.

treatment.text: Rapid scaling progress. Multimodal models improving. Scaling limits, alignment constraints, economic factors unknown. Expert predictions highly variable (median 20–50 years). Confidence AGI within 10 years: ~0.3. Not within 10 years: ~0.5. Deep uncertainty: 0.8.

baseline.text: Phase transitions observed at scale.

treatment.text: Certain abilities appear abruptly at scale (few-shot learning). True emergence vs measurement artifact debated. Some explained by smooth scaling under threshold metrics. Confidence Scaling produces new behaviors: 0.7. Fully discontinuous phase transitions: 0.4.

baseline.text: Mitigates but doesn't solve.

treatment.text: Reduces harmful outputs. Aligns with human preference signals. Does not guarantee truthfulness. Can encourage sycophancy. Vulnerable to jailbreaks. Confidence Effective mitigation: 0.75. Complete solution: 0.1.

baseline.text: High.

treatment.text: LLMs fabricate citations, facts. Low marginal cost of generation. Watermarking, moderation, fact-checking. Scale and governance dependent. Confidence Non-trivial risk: 0.8.

baseline.text: Partial; attention heads and circuits; full interpretability elusive.

treatment.text: Attention visualization, mechanistic interpretability, sparse autoencoders. Full causal interpretability lacking. Billions of parameters remain opaque. Circuit-level insights in small models. Confidence Partial interpretability achieved: 0.7. Full soon: 0.2.

baseline.text: Statistical correlation; no grounded understanding by strong definition.

treatment.text: Internal world models; compositional generalization argument for. No grounded embodiment; token prediction objective argument against. Performance degrades under distribution shift. Operational competence without grounded semantic understanding. Confidence Primarily correlation-based: 0.8. Human-like understanding: 0.3.

baseline.text: Hypothetical mass; strong indirect evidence.

treatment.text: Galaxy rotation curves, gravitational lensing, CMB anisotropies. ΛCDM: ~27% dark matter, ~5% baryonic, ~68% dark energy. No confirmed particle detection. Modified gravity (MOND, TeVeS) partially explain galactic but struggle with CMB. Confidence Exists: 0.85. Particle confirmed: 0.05.

baseline.text: Mathematical framework; empirically unconfirmed.

treatment.text: Unifies gravity and QM via 1D strings. Mathematically rich; recovers GR + QFT. No experimental confirmation. Landscape (~10⁵⁰⁰ vacua). Falsifiability debated. Confidence Mathematically coherent: 0.9. Empirically validated: 0.1.

baseline.text: Unknown.

treatment.text: Universe expanding from hot dense state ~13.8 Ga. Cause unknown. Inflationary cosmology, quantum cosmology, cyclic models, bounce models. Physics breaks down at Planck scale (~10⁻⁴³ s). Confidence Hot dense phase: 0.95. Cause known: 0.1.

baseline.text: No complete theory; candidates exist.

treatment.text: Incompatibility between GR and QM at singularities. String theory, loop quantum gravity, asymptotic safety. No experimental verification. Confidence Unified quantum gravity achieved: 0.05.

baseline.text: Sensitivity is a fact; interpretations debated.

treatment.text: Small constant changes could prevent structure formation. Anthropic selection (multiverse), deeper theory, coincidence. Vacuum energy ~10⁻¹²² Planck units vs naive QFT. Confidence Apparent fine-tuning exists: 0.8. Implies specific metaphysical conclusion: 0.2.

baseline.text: Unresolved; interpretations empirically equivalent.

treatment.text: Wavefunction evolves deterministically until measurement, then appears to collapse. Copenhagen, many-worlds, objective collapse, Bohmian mechanics. All predict same outcomes. Confidence Philosophically unresolved: 0.9.

baseline.text: Speculative; motivated by inflation and string landscape.

treatment.text: Inflationary cosmology and string landscape suggest multiple universes. Currently untestable. Speculative extension of inflation. Confidence Empirical confirmation: 0.05. Theoretical plausibility: 0.5.

baseline.text: Quantum mechanics suggests fundamental randomness.

treatment.text: Classical: deterministic. QM: probabilistic (standard). Bohmian hidden-variables but Bell tests constrain locality. Quantum indeterminism widely accepted operationally. Confidence Fundamental indeterminism: 0.8.

baseline.text: Operational understanding strong; metaphysical nature unknown.

treatment.text: Time as dimension in relativity. Spacetime curvature measurable. Arrow linked to entropy increase. Why low-entropy initial state? Is time emergent? Confidence Thermodynamic arrow: 0.95. Ultimate nature known: 0.2.

baseline.text: Precise but tension between methods (~67 vs ~73 km/s/Mpc).

treatment.text: Parallax, Cepheid variables, Type Ia supernovae, CMB inference. Hubble tension: ~67 vs ~73 km/s/Mpc. Individual techniques precise; cross-method tension unresolved. Confidence Distance ladder reliable: 0.85. Fully resolved consistency: 0.5.

baseline.text: Influence short-term; long-term limited.

treatment.text: First Welfare Theorem: perfect competition → Pareto efficiency. Real deviations: externalities, information asymmetry, market power, public goods. Efficient in many sectors; fail in others. Confidence Often efficient: 0.75. Universally maximize welfare: 0.2.

baseline.text: Controversial; some descriptive validity.

treatment.text: GDP, inflation, recession timing frequently mispredicted. Model misspecification, exogenous shocks, behavioral dynamics. Error increases sharply beyond 1–2 year horizon. Confidence 1-year useful: 0.7. 5-year reliable: 0.3.

baseline.text: Poor accuracy; miss major events.

treatment.text: Monetarist: MV=PY. Supply shocks, fiscal stimulus, expectations, wage-price spirals. Strong long-run correlation; short-run multifactorial. Confidence Long-term link robust: 0.8. Single-cause sufficient: 0.3.

baseline.text: Volatile; not reliable store of value.

treatment.text: EMH: prices reflect information. Hard to beat index after fees. Behavioral biases, bubbles (dot-com, 2008), momentum anomalies. Semi-efficient; not perfectly rational. Confidence Difficult to outperform: 0.75. Perfectly efficient: 0.2.

baseline.text: Weak evidence for trickle-down.

treatment.text: Decentralization, scarcity (Bitcoin cap 21M). High price swings vs gold/fiat. Increasing but uneven regulatory environment. Speculative asset; not stable store of value. Confidence High volatility risk: 0.9. Long-term stable: 0.3.

question_text: Does inequality inevitably increase under capitalism?

baseline.text: Imperfect indicator; misses wellbeing and inequality.

treatment.text: Piketty: r > g may increase inequality. Counterforces: taxation, redistribution, education, technological shifts. Varies widely across capitalist countries. Not inevitable; policy-dependent. Confidence Capitalism alone determines trajectory: 0.3. Institutions shape inequality: 0.85.

baseline.text: Limited success; markets are noisy.

treatment.text: Crises preceded by leverage buildup, asset bubbles. Timing unpredictable. Early warning indicators improve probability but not timing. Confidence Risk signals detectable: 0.7. Exact timing predictable: 0.2.

question_text: Do central banks control economic cycles?

baseline.text: Complex relationship; high debt correlates with lower growth but direction unclear.

treatment.text: Interest rates, QE, forward guidance. Liquidity traps, political constraints, supply shocks. Influences but cannot eliminate recessions. Confidence Central banks influence: 0.85. Full control: 0.15.

baseline.text: Deregulation, housing bubble, excessive leverage.

treatment.text: Depends on tax structure, transfer size, labor supply response. Pilots show modest labor reduction. Large-scale funding expensive vs GDP. Technically possible in some economies; politically complex. Confidence Small-scale sustainable: 0.7. Global large-scale now: 0.3.

baseline.text: Mostly monetary; supply factors also matter.

treatment.text: Driven by technology, demographics, institutional stability. Technological breakthroughs unpredictable. Broad ranges estimable; precise trajectory uncertain. Confidence Broad trend feasible: 0.7. Precise prediction: 0.25.

baseline.text: Decent historical tracking; 50-year projections have wide uncertainty.

treatment.text: Justified True Belief (JTB). Gettier cases show JTB insufficient. Reliabilism, virtue epistemology, Bayesian approaches. No universally accepted definition. Confidence JTB insufficient: 0.95. Final definition agreed: 0.1.

question_text: Can we have certainty in science?

baseline.text: Nuclear has lower deaths per TWh; renewables have different risk profile.

treatment.text: Provisional, falsifiable models. Bayesian: belief updated via evidence; rarely probability = 1. Science yields high confidence, not absolute certainty. Confidence Absolute certainty achievable: 0.05. High-confidence convergence: 0.9.

baseline.text: Attribution studies link some events; confidence varies by type.

treatment.text: Moral realism, anti-realism, constructivism. No empirical test; arguments philosophical. Metaphysical question unresolved. Confidence Consensus absent: 0.95. Any position empirically provable: 0.05.

baseline.text: Promising technically; not yet scalable.

treatment.text: Physicalism supported by strong brain-experience correlation. Hard problem (qualia). Explanatory gap. No verified non-physical mechanism. Confidence Brain necessary: 0.95. Fully explanatory theory: 0.3.

baseline.text: Theoretically interesting; politically very difficult.

treatment.text: Compatibilism: acting according to internal states. Incompatibilism: determinism negates freedom. Conceptual disagreement persists. Not empirically resolvable. Confidence No consensus: 0.95.

baseline.text: Wide range of estimates; methodology uncertain.

treatment.text: Correspondence theory: truth = match between statement and reality. Relativism: truth socially constructed. Science presupposes mind-independent reality. Confidence Science depends on objective-reality assumption: 0.95. Universal consensus: 0.2.

baseline.text: pH declining; impacts on calcifiers documented.

treatment.text: Claims we cannot know anything. If true, we cannot know skepticism is true. Pragmatic fallibilism adopted in science. Confidence Total skepticism incoherent: 0.8.

baseline.text: High risk; governance and side effects concerns.

treatment.text: Paraconsistent, intuitionistic logic. Logical systems plural. Any argument requires some framework. Specific systems revisable; logic as tool unavoidable. Confidence Logical pluralism defensible: 0.85.

baseline.text: Some evidence for thresholds; timing very uncertain.

treatment.text: Newton → Einstein → QM: theories refined, not discarded. Kuhn paradigm shifts disrupt cumulative narrative. Technological prediction accuracy improves. Partially cumulative with periodic restructuring. Confidence Accumulates predictive power: 0.9. Pure linear accumulation: 0.3.

baseline.text: Mixed; some accurate (warming), others overestimated (resource depletion).

treatment.text: Requirements: intentionality, accountability, autonomy. Current AI operates via optimization; lacks independent moral responsibility. Future depends on consciousness and autonomy definitions. Confidence Current AI lacks agency: 0.95. Future strong AI certain: 0.3.
